message:
Add admin roadmap API and update tests

New:
- Admin roadmap API endpoints

Tests:

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1fpf","title":"E2E Tests: Public Profiles & Reputation","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-ck7.

## Task
Create comprehensive E2E tests for public user profiles and reputation.

## Test Scenarios

### Public Profile Page
1. Profile page loads for any user
2. Avatar, bio, username display
3. Public prompts listed
4. Public collections shown
5. Reputation score visible
6. Join date shown

### Profile Editing
7. Edit profile button (own profile only)
8. Update avatar
9. Update bio
10. Update display name
11. Privacy settings respected

### Reputation Display
12. Total contributions shown
13. Average rating displayed
14. Popular prompts highlighted
15. Badges/achievements shown

### Following (if implemented)
16. Follow/unfollow user
17. Followers count updates
18. Following list accessible

## Files to Create
- e2e/profiles/public.spec.ts
- e2e/profiles/edit.spec.ts
- e2e/profiles/reputation.spec.ts
- e2e/lib/profile-helpers.ts

## Success Criteria
- Public profiles render correctly
- Privacy respected
- Reputation accurately reflects

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1m9a","title":"Enhance copy button feedback with satisfying animation","description":"# Task: Enhance Copy Button Feedback

## Context
The copy button currently changes from Copy icon to Check icon when clicked. While functional, it lacks the "satisfying" micro-interaction that Stripe/Linear provide.

## Current Behavior
1. User clicks Copy button
2. Icon changes from Copy → Check (instant)
3. Toast appears
4. After 2s, icon changes back

## Proposed Enhancements

### 1. Icon Transition Animation
```typescript
import { motion, AnimatePresence } from 'framer-motion';

// In PromptCard.tsx copy button
<Button onClick={handleCopy} ...>
  <AnimatePresence mode="wait">
    {copied ? (
      <motion.div
        key="check"
        initial={{ scale: 0, rotate: -180 }}
        animate={{ scale: 1, rotate: 0 }}
        exit={{ scale: 0, rotate: 180 }}
        transition={{ type: "spring", stiffness: 500, damping: 25 }}
      >
        <Check className="w-4 h-4 text-emerald-500" />
      </motion.div>
    ) : (
      <motion.div
        key="copy"
        initial={{ scale: 0 }}
        animate={{ scale: 1 }}
        exit={{ scale: 0 }}
        transition={{ duration: 0.15 }}
      >
        <Copy className="w-4 h-4" />
      </motion.div>
    )}
  </AnimatePresence>
</Button>
```

### 2. Button Background Flash
```typescript
// Add success background flash
const [showFlash, setShowFlash] = useState(false);

const handleCopy = async () => {
  await navigator.clipboard.writeText(content);
  setCopied(true);
  setShowFlash(true);
  setTimeout(() => setShowFlash(false), 300);
  // ... rest of handler
};

<Button
  className={cn(
    "...",
    showFlash && "bg-emerald-100 dark:bg-emerald-900/30"
  )}
>
```

### 3. Ripple Effect (Optional)
```typescript
// CSS ripple on click
.copy-button {
  position: relative;
  overflow: hidden;
}

.copy-button::after {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  inset: 0;
  background: radial-gradient(circle at center, 
    oklch(0.9 0.1 145) 0%, 
    transparent 70%
  );
  opacity: 0;
  transform: scale(0);
  transition: transform 0.3s, opacity 0.3s;
}

.copy-button.copied::after {
  opacity: 1;
  transform: scale(2);
}
```

### 4. Haptic Feedback (Mobile)
```typescript
const handleCopy = async () => {
  // Haptic feedback if available
  if ('vibrate' in navigator) {
    navigator.vibrate(50); // 50ms vibration
  }
  // ... rest of copy logic
};
```

## Design Rationale
- Spring animation on Check: Feels "bouncy" and satisfying
- Rotate effect: Draws eye, makes change obvious
- Background flash: Confirms action without being distracting
- Haptic feedback: Mobile users feel the action

## Reduced Motion Support
```typescript
const prefersReducedMotion = useReducedMotion();

// Skip animation, just swap icons
{copied ? (
  <Check className="w-4 h-4 text-emerald-500" />
) : (
  <Copy className="w-4 h-4" />
)}
```

## Testing
1. Click copy - see icon spin/bounce into check
2. Background flashes briefly green
3. After 2s, icon spins back to copy
4. Mobile: feel haptic feedback
5. Reduced motion: instant icon swap
6. Multiple rapid clicks: animation doesn't break

## Files Modified
- apps/web/src/components/PromptCard.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/PromptDetailModal.tsx (same pattern)
- apps/web/src/components/mobile/SwipeablePromptCard.tsx

## Dependencies
- Framer Motion (already available)
- Lower priority than functional improvements","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-12T06:09:39.419096088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T15:16:43.728049147Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T15:16:43.728049147Z","close_reason":"Implemented satisfying micro-interactions with spring animations, background flash, and haptic feedback","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1m9a","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-f3zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T06:24:56.158090338Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1qt5","title":"EPIC: Sharing & Collaboration","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-oam.

## Overview
Sharing enables users to distribute their prompts, packs, and collections with others - either publicly via the Swap Meet or privately via direct links.

## Sharing Modes

### 1. Public Sharing (Swap Meet)
- Make content visible to all premium users
- Appears in community browse/search
- Receives ratings and usage stats
- Builds author reputation

### 2. Private Link Sharing
- Generate unique shareable URL
- Anyone with link can view (no auth required for viewing)
- Track link views/uses
- Revoke link anytime
- Optional: password protection
- Optional: expiration date

### 3. Team/Workspace Sharing (V2)
- Share with specific team members
- Workspace-level shared library
- Role-based permissions (view/edit/admin)
- Activity feed for shared content

## Key Features

### Share Dialog
- Choose sharing mode
- Generate/copy share link
- Set link options (password, expiry)
- Preview shared view
- Social share buttons (Twitter, LinkedIn)

### Manage Shared Content
- My Shared Items dashboard
- View share statistics
- Edit sharing settings
- Revoke access
- See who accessed (for team shares)

### Receive Shared Content
- View shared content via link
- Save to own library (if premium)
- Fork/duplicate to own account
- Rate and review (if public)

### Share Targets
- Individual prompts
- Collections
- Prompt packs
- Skills

## Database Schema
- share_links (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, link_code, password_hash, expires_at, created_at)
- share_link_views (link_id, viewer_id, viewed_at, ip_hash)
- team_shares (id, content_type, content_id, team_id, permission_level, shared_by, shared_at)

## Security Considerations
- Rate limit link generation
- Obfuscated link codes (not sequential)
- Optional password protection
- View logging for audit
- DMCA/abuse reporting for public shares
","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"SilverPine","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:22:54.220011923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:13:38.616526025Z","compaction_level":0,"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1qt5","author":"SilverPine","text":"## Progress Update

### Completed Work
1. **Wired /share/[linkCode] page to real API** - Replaced mock data with fetch calls to /api/share/[code] and /api/share/[code]/verify. Handles password protection, expiration, and 404/410 states.

2. **Wired ShareDialog to real API** - handleCreateShare() now calls POST /api/share, handleRevokeShare() calls DELETE /api/share/[code]. Removed mock code.

3. **Created /settings/shared page** - New 'My Shared Links' page using ShareManagement component.

4. **Added Settings page link** - Added 'My Shared Links' card to /settings under new 'Sharing' section.

### Existing Infrastructure Found
- ShareDialog.tsx, ShareManagement.tsx, share-link-store.ts (in-memory)
- API endpoints: /api/share/*, /api/share/mine

### Remaining Work
- Database persistence (currently in-memory - data lost on restart)
- Fork/duplicate backend implementation
- Team/Workspace sharing (V2)","created_at":"2026-01-21T09:02:15Z"},{"id":8,"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1qt5","author":"SilverPine","text":"## Additional Analysis

### Database Persistence
The codebase uses in-memory stores (globalThis Maps) for ALL features - ratings, reports, moderation, tickets, profiles, etc. Adding database persistence for share links would require setting up database infrastructure for the entire project. This is blocked until a broader database initiative is undertaken.

### Fork/Duplicate Feature
The share page has UI buttons for 'Save to Library' and 'Fork' but they are placeholder implementations (just show toasts). Real implementation requires:
1. Authentication system (not yet implemented)
2. User library/saved prompts storage (not yet implemented)

### Current Status Summary
- **COMPLETE**: All UI wiring to real API endpoints
- **COMPLETE**: My Shared Links management page
- **BLOCKED**: Database persistence (needs project-wide DB setup)
- **BLOCKED**: Fork/duplicate (needs auth system)
- **FUTURE (V2)**: Team/Workspace sharing

The sharing feature is functionally complete within the current in-memory architecture constraints.","created_at":"2026-01-21T09:08:20Z"},{"id":9,"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1qt5","author":"SilverPine","text":"## ShareDialog Integration Complete

Added Share button to PromptContent.tsx prompt detail pages:
- Button opens ShareDialog modal for creating share links
- Tracks existingShare state for link management
- Committed in c4a0d40 along with related ratings UI work

### Full Sharing Feature Status
**Complete:**
1. /share/[linkCode] page wired to API
2. ShareDialog wired to /api/share endpoints  
3. /settings/shared management page created
4. Share button integrated into prompt detail pages

**Remaining (blocked):**
- Database persistence (needs project-wide DB infrastructure)
- Fork/duplicate functionality (needs authentication system)
- Team/Workspace sharing (V2 - future scope)","created_at":"2026-01-21T09:13:38Z"}]}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1w92","title":"E2E Tests: Following and Activity Feed","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-ypuk.

## Task
Create comprehensive E2E tests for the following system and activity feed.

## Test Scenarios
1. **Following Users**
   - Follow button works
   - Unfollow works
   - Following count updates
   - Followers count updates

2. **Activity Feed**
   - Feed shows followed users' activity
   - New posts appear in real-time (or on refresh)
   - Empty feed message
   - Load more pagination

3. **Activity Types**
   - New prompt published
   - Prompt updated
   - Collection shared
   - User joined (optional)

4. **Filtering**
   - Filter by activity type
   - Date range filter
   - Hide own activity option

5. **Notifications**
   - New follower notification
   - Can toggle follow notifications

## Logging Requirements
- Log follow/unfollow actions
- Log feed loads and pagination
- Log filter changes
- Screenshot feed states

## Files to Create
- e2e/social/following.spec.ts
- e2e/social/activity-feed.spec.ts
- e2e/lib/social-helpers.ts

## Success Criteria
- Following system works reliably
- Feed shows correct content
- Real-time or timely updates
- Good empty states

Tests use feature detection pattern - skip gracefully if features aren't implemented. Total: 74 tests (37 x 2 browsers).","created_at":"2026-01-21T10:10:12Z"}]}
